# @spr-networks/plugin-ui

Frontend kit for SPR router plugins. A plugin UI is a Create React App (react-scripts +
craco) bundled to a single self-contained build/index.html, served by the plugin's Go
backend, and embedded by the SPR app in an iframe. This package provides the SPR look,
theme sync with the host, an authenticated API client, and UI primitives.

Import EVERYTHING from this one package. It re-exports all of @gluestack-ui/themed
(Box, VStack, HStack, Button, ButtonText, Input, Badge, Switch, Spinner, useToast, ...)
plus the SPR-specific exports below. Never import '@gluestack-ui/themed' directly.
Note: `Card` from this package is the SPR-styled card and intentionally overrides
gluestack's Card.

## Entry point

import React from 'react'
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'
import { PluginApp } from '@spr-networks/plugin-ui'
import Plugin from './Plugin'

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render(
  <PluginApp><Plugin /></PluginApp>
)

PluginApp = GluestackUIProvider preconfigured with SPR tokens + automatic theme sync
with the host (light/dark and custom theme colors, via window.SPR_THEME and postMessage
{type:'spr:theme'}) + AlertProvider for toasts. Optional prop: onMessage(data, event)
to observe non-theme messages from the host.

## API client

import { api, API } from '@spr-networks/plugin-ui'

api.get('/plugins/<uri>/config')      -> Promise<parsed json>
api.put('/plugins/<uri>/config', obj) -> Promise
api.post(url, obj), api.delete(url)
api.pluginURI()                       -> the plugin's URI string from the host
api.getApiURL()                       -> SPR API base URL

Subclass for a plugin's own base path:
class MyAPI extends API {
  constructor() { super(`/plugins/${api.pluginURI()}/`) }
  config() { return this.get('config') }
  save(c) { return this.put('config', c) }
}

Requests are authenticated automatically (host-injected globals + SPR session).
Rejections have shape { message, status, response }.

## Alerts (toasts)

import { useAlert } from '@spr-networks/plugin-ui'
const alert = useAlert()
alert.success('Saved')
alert.error('Failed to save', err)     // second arg optional: string or Error
alert.warning(...), alert.info(...)
Works anywhere under PluginApp.

## Layout

<Page>...</Page>                 whole-view container: padding, content background,
                                 centered max-width column, min-height 100vh
<ListHeader title description>   page/section heading; children render on the right
  <Button .../>
</ListHeader>
<ListItem>...</ListItem>         card-styled row with bottom border
<EmptyState icon title description>children</EmptyState>
<Loading text />                 centered spinner

## Cards + stats

<Card>...</Card>                              SPR surface card (rounded, bordered)
<SectionHeader title count right={<node>} />  heading row inside a card
<StatTile label value mono />                 metric cell; wrap several in
                                              <HStack flexWrap="wrap" gap="$2">
<KeyVal label value mono />                   label/value row
<StatusDot online warn />                     green/amber/muted status dot
<Toggle value onPress disabled />             pill switch

## Forms

<TextField label value onChangeText placeholder helper error secureTextEntry />
<ModalForm isOpen onClose title>form children</ModalForm>
<ModalConfirm isOpen onClose onConfirm title message confirmText destructive />

## Utilities

timeAgo(ts)   humanize an RFC3339 timestamp ('30s ago', '5m ago', '3h ago', '2d ago');
              returns null (render as em dash) for empty/unparseable values and Go's
              zero time 0001-01-01T00:00:00Z ("never")

## Styling rules

- Use gluestack tokens: p="$4", space="md", bg="$backgroundCardLight", color="$muted500"
- Dark mode via sx: sx={{ _dark: { bg: '$backgroundCardDark' } }}
- Do not hardcode hex colors; the host theme overrides tokens at runtime
- primary = SPR brand ramp, muted = neutral ramp; card surfaces use
  backgroundCardLight/Dark + borderColorCardLight/Dark, page background uses
  backgroundContentLight/Dark

## Build scaffolding (template/)

template/index.js        entry rendering <PluginApp><Plugin/></PluginApp>
template/Plugin.js       worked example: load config, cards, stats, form, confirm
template/craco.config.js inlines the bundle into one index.html and transpiles
                         @gluestack-ui, @gluestack-style, @legendapp and this package
template/bundle.sh       npx craco build + inline step -> build/index.html
template/public/index.html

Plugin package.json needs: react, react-dom, react-scripts 5, @craco/craco,
@gluestack-ui/themed 1.1.3, @gluestack-ui/config 1.1.2, @gluestack-style/react 1.0.45,
react-native-web, react-native-svg, babel-plugin-react-native-web, and this package via
git URL: "@spr-networks/plugin-ui": "git+https://github.com/spr-networks/spr-plugin-ui.git"

Pin the gluestack versions EXACTLY (no ^ ranges): these are the versions the SPR app
ships, and newer 1.0.5x gluestack-style releases change colorMode handling. Do not wrap
the app in React.StrictMode: gluestack-style's StyledProvider identifies the root
provider by comparing a module-level id against useId(), and StrictMode's double render
breaks the match, so dark mode is never applied.

## Host integration contract (for reference)

- Production: SPR fetches the plugin's index.html, injects window.SPR_API_URL,
  window.SPR_PLUGIN = {Name, URI, GitURL}, window.SPR_THEME = {colorMode, theme, colors},
  and renders it as iframe srcDoc. colors is a flat {gluestackToken: '#hex'} map.
- Dev: iframe src=http://localhost:8080?colorMode=light|dark
- Live theme changes: host posts JSON {type:'spr:theme', colorMode, theme, colors}
- On mount the kit posts {type:'spr:ready'} to the host, which re-sends the theme
